title: RUN --mount=type=cache leads to AVC denial
body:
### Issue Description

Building this Containerfile fails on Fedora 38 workstation with enforcing selinux:
```
FROM gradle:8.1.1-jdk17
WORKDIR /usr/app
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/app/build-cache touch build.gradle.kts && echo "buildCache { local { directory = File(rootDir, \"build-cache\") } }" > settings.gradle.kts && gradle --no-daemon --build-cache
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Build the dockerfile with `podman build .`
2. It doesn't build
3. Cry


### Describe the results you received

```
STEP 1/3: FROM gradle:8.1.1-jdk17
STEP 2/3: WORKDIR /usr/app
--> Using cache 367a5d89aeab1543c7e44dbb6eb3ac77691f8ebbc3cec883660cadda35cd41e7
--> 367a5d89aeab
STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/app/build-cache touch build.gradle.kts && echo "buildCache { local { directory = File(rootDir, \"build-cache\") } }" > settings.gradle.kts && gradle --no-daemon --build-cache

Welcome to Gradle 8.1.1!

Here are the highlights of this release:
 - Stable configuration cache
 - Experimental Kotlin DSL assignment syntax
 - Building with Java 20

For more details see https://docs.gradle.org/8.1.1/release-notes.html

To honour the JVM settings for this build a single-use Daemon process will be forked. See https://docs.gradle.org/8.1.1/userguide/gradle_daemon.html#sec:disabling_the_daemon.
Daemon will be stopped at the end of the build 

FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:
A problem occurred configuring root project 'app'.
> Failed to load cache entry e3befb54606bbe3aef56e241c46661c8 for Kotlin DSL plugin specs accessors for classpath 'd909f700203a555b04ff6d876cea0618': Could not load from local cache: /usr/app/build-cache/e3befb54606bbe3aef56e241c46661c8 (Permission denied)

* Try:
> Run with --stacktrace option to get the stack trace.
> Run with --info or --debug option to get more log output.
> Run with --scan to get full insights.

* Get more help at https://help.gradle.org

BUILD FAILED in 28s
Error: building at STEP "RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/app/build-cache touch build.gradle.kts && echo "buildCache { local { directory = File(rootDir, \"build-cache\") } }" > settings.gradle.kts && gradle --no-daemon --build-cache": while running runtime: exit status 1
```

audit.log contains:
```
type=AVC msg=audit(1687587359.801:235): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=7530 comm=4461656D6F6E20776F726B6572 name="e3befb54606bbe3aef56e241c46661c8" dev="dm-0" ino=9552496 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c350,c898 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c71,c285 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1687587359.801:236): avc:  denied  { rename } for  pid=7530 comm=4461656D6F6E20776F726B6572 name="e3befb54606bbe3aef56e241c46661c8" dev="dm-0" ino=9552496 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c350,c898 tcontext=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c71,c285 tclass=file permissive=0
```

In other words, the cache at `/usr/app/build-cache` is labelled as `:Z` when it should be `:z`.

### Describe the results you expected

Image builds

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.5.1
API Version:  4.5.1
Go Version:   go1.20.4
Built:        Fri May 26 18:58:48 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
podman-4.5.1-1.fc38.x86_64
container-selinux-2.218.0-1.fc38.noarch
```

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: This would be a bug in Buildah not Podman.

That is not what I am seeing?

```
$ id -u
3267
$ rm -rf /var/tmp/buildah-cache-3267/
$ podman build --no-cache /tmp/dan/
STEP 1/4: FROM fedora
STEP 2/4: WORKDIR /usr/app
--> 2cc101ce3bc6
STEP 3/4: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/app/build-cache touch /usr/app/build-cache/dan
--> e06cb12fbbab
STEP 4/4: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/app/build-cache ls -Z /usr/app/build-cache/dan
system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0 /usr/app/build-cache/dan
COMMIT
--> e88cb7a2144b
e88cb7a2144bfae393fb8ed611427624b5f47a328b2cbfb0796796892ae5ef1d
```

It was probably an issue with unclean cache or something. Anyway, I started to use a volume instead because it is mostly built in CI and I wanted cross-job caching. I'm closing this as you couldn't reproduce it and I no longer want to.
